mnemonics.title: UVXYZ Sequence
mnemonics.content: Underwater Vehicles eXamine Yellow Zebras (UVXYZ) - Remember the string division order: u, v, x, y, z

mnemonics.title: Pumping Conditions
mnemonics.content: P.L. = Pump Limits: 1) Pump any i, 2) vy ≠ ε, 3) |vxy| ≤ p. Remember 3 conditions through PL initials

mnemonics.title: Proof Strategy
mnemonics.content: S.C.R.A.M - Select string, Choose divisions, Reach contradiction, Apply pumping, Maintain conditions

mnemonics.title: Non-Empty Check
mnemonics.content: VY Not Zero - At least one of v or y must be non-empty (|vy| > 0)

acronyms.usage: Remember the pumping length p is determined by the grammar
Recall the third condition |vxy| ≤ p focuses on the middle segment
Remember pumping works for any iteration count i ≥ 0

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tall parse tree with a repeated non-terminal node - the subtree between these repetitions represents v and y
Picture a string as 5 colored sections: u(blue), v(red), x(green), y(red), z(blue) - red sections get pumped together
Visualize p as a yardstick - any string longer than p must have pumpable segments within the first p symbols
See the pumping process as a ladder - if any rung (pumped string) falls outside L, the whole structure collapses

analogies.analogy: Like a bicycle pump - pushing (i>1) or pulling (i=0) the v and y sections maintains the 'air pressure' (structure) of the string
Similar to a fractal pattern - repeating the same production rules creates self-similar sections (v and y) that can be duplicated/removed
Like trying to fit a square peg in a round hole - if pumping distorts essential patterns, the language isn't context-free
